title: Hitt's Hill Park - Seattle, WA
content: May 18, 2014 - 1 review and 2 photos of HITT'S HILL PARK "Hitt's Hill Park is a tiny little park not far from the hustle and bustle of Columbia City's Rainier Ave. There's one main trail that takes you through some lovely woods. You would never know it, but this spot used to be the site of the Hitt Brothers

title: Hitt's Hill Park - Project Nature
content: November 9, 2018 - Hitt’s Hill is a 3.2-acre wooded hilltop on the southwest end of Columbia City that was once home to the Hitt’s Fireworks factory. Thomas Gabriel Hitt founded the Hitt Brothers Firework Company on the hill in about 1905. From the top of the hill, it is possible to view Lake Washington, downtown Seattle and the Rainier …

title: Hitt's Hill Park - Parks | seattle.gov
content: From the top of the hill, it is possible to view Lake Washington, Mt. Baker, downtown Seattle and the Rainier Valley. The family-run fireworks company is credited with creating pyrotechnics for the movie "Gone With the Wind," as well as providing fireworks for shows at Green Lake and Ivar's Fourth of July celebrations through the 1970s. Hitt Fireworks Company closed forever, in 1976.

title: Hitt's Hill Park | Recplanet.com
content: Welcome to Seattle's Hitt's Hill Park, the birthplace of the Hitts Firework Company which was founded by Thomas Gabriel Hitt. To contact the park office please call 206-684-4075.
